title: Skills that make vibe coding work
summary: Provisional: fundamentals courses, trap warnings, and year-long builder retrospectives define the skills conversation.
synthesisHtml:
🌐 last30days v3.8.2 · synced 2026-07-23 · provisional

What I learned (from Ranked Evidence Clusters - full synthesis pending):

Fundamentals still lead the skills feed. Top clusters include Tina Huang's "Vibe Coding Fundamentals," long Gemini/Antigravity courses, Claude's official AI courses, and Anthropic's free engineer masterclass - people want structured skill paths, not vibes alone.

The counter-skill is knowing the trap. HN/YouTube titles like "The Vibe Coding Trap," "Vibe Coding is a Trap (What Senior Devs See)," and "Who cleans up after the vibe-coding party?" sit next to showcase posts - judgment and cleanup are framed as skills.

Practitioners publish year-one retrospectives. Reddit clusters ("I have been vibe coding for more than a year," "when my opinion of vibe coding changed," "How to make vibe coding not suck") treat taste, scope control, and professional AI use as the real skill stack. pinkycollie/awesome-vibe-coding and mattpocock/skills show up as GitHub anchors.

KEY PATTERNS:

  1. Learn fundamentals before speed - courses and ABCs posts still outrank pure demos.
  2. Trap-awareness is a skill - failure-mode content is part of the curriculum.
  3. Retrospectives beat tips - multi-project builders set the bar for "what actually works."
  4. Skills packs are becoming portable - awesome lists and mattpocock/skills-style repos package reusable agent skills.
keyPatterns: **Learn fundamentals before speed** - courses and ABCs posts still outrank pure demos.
**Trap-awareness is a skill** - failure-mode content is part of the curriculum.
**Retrospectives beat tips** - multi-project builders set the bar for "what actually works."
**Skills packs are becoming portable** - awesome lists and mattpocock/skills-style repos package reusable agent skills.
